outlook.headline: 200-layer NAND demand stays constructive as investment recovers
outlook.summary: Both Lam and Tokyo Electron frame 200-layer NAND as a positive transition: higher-layer stacks expand tool content for Lam, while TEL sees recovering 3D NAND investment and tight supply supporting process-tool demand. The near-term read is improving, though mentions are flat to slightly down, so breadth is not yet accelerating. Watch whether NAND capex and layer-count ramps broaden beyond these two suppliers.

drivers.label: Higher-layer stack transition expands tool content
drivers.detail: 200-layer NAND adds process complexity and increases content per wafer, which is constructive for equipment demand.

drivers.label: 3D NAND investment recovery
drivers.detail: A rebound in 3D NAND spending points to improving order momentum for next-layer NAND processing tools.

drivers.label: Tight supply supports process demand
drivers.detail: Tighter supply in the NAND chain can keep conversion and capacity-add spend elevated near term.

risks: NAND capex recovery could stall if memory makers delay layer-count ramps or capacity additions.
Flat-to-lower mention trend suggests the theme is not yet broadening beyond a narrow set of suppliers.
Any weakening in 3D NAND pricing or demand could dampen the recovery in investment.
